title: asyneuma limonifolium subf. ramosum
intro: Asyneuma limonifolium subf. ramosum, a lesser-known subspecies of the Asyneuma limonifolium plant, is a type of flowering perennial plant native to the Himalayas. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Asyneuma limonifolium subf. ramosum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin infections. As for herbal preparations, the plant is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be obtained by boiling the leaves and roots of the plant in water.
